evidence.note: The Wikipedia article on pipelining in computing confirms that the 7-stage pipeline is a fundamental component of modern computer architecture.
The PDF document from the University of Alaska Fairbanks provides an in-depth explanation of the 7-stage pipeline in computer architecture.
The IEEE paper discusses the evolution of pipeline architectures, but does not specifically focus on the 7-stage pipeline.
The ResearchGate publication mentions the development of new pipeline architectures, implying changes to the 7-stage pipeline, but lacks concrete evidence.
The ACM Digital Library paper discusses the limitations of the 7-stage pipeline, including issues with branch prediction and data dependencies.
The ScienceDirect article examines the limitations of pipelining in general, which applies to the 7-stage pipeline.
The arXiv paper proposes a novel pipeline architecture that addresses the limitations of the 7-stage pipeline.
The USENIX ATC '20 paper presents an alternative pipeline design that improves performance and efficiency.
